etymology.description: 𠀠 (basket) -> 𠔝 with added 丌 (stand, phonetic) -> borrowed as pronoun
etymology.interpretation: Originally depicted a basket (𠀠), which later included a stand (丌) beneath it to stabilize the image on bronze inscriptions, forming 𠔝. This combination was later borrowed for its sound to represent the pronoun 'his, her, its', and the basket meaning was reassigned to 箕(箕) (jī, 'basket').

examples.chinese: 他把其思绪整理了一遍(他把其思緒整理了一遍)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bǎ qí sīxù zhěnglǐ le yī biàn.

examples.chinese: 其琴声如水般柔软(其琴聲如水般柔軟)。
examples.pinyin: Qí qín shēng rú shuǐ bān róuruǎn.
examples.english: His playing is as soft as water.

examples.chinese: 其计划得到了同事们的支持(其計劃得到了同事們的支持)。
examples.pinyin: Qí jìhuà dédào le tóngshìmen de zhīchí.
examples.english: His plan received the support of his colleagues.
